meta.title: 5. Safety and Protection with Bryan Singer
meta.description:
Bryan Singer starts with the quote "when good systems go bad" ... this is when safety or protection systems prevent a catastrophe. It is a key lesson for understanding risk.

Apologies for not having the video of Bryan, but you have his voice and the presentation on the screen 100% of the time. This is an awesome presentation from someone who has seen and been hands on in 100's of plants.

Questions to consider and discuss:

1) Why don't you see SIS (Safety Instrumented Systems) in a large percentage of plants for safety?

2) Why would you not want to integrate your safety and control system together?

3) What is the difference between fail energized (open) and fail closed? Which would result in a larger consequence?
